markdown:
#  What are the default columns in the Contracts tab of the Commitments tool? 
##  Answer 
By default, the following columns are available in the Contracts tab of the project's Commitments tool. While you can't add or remove columns from the tool, you can choose which columns to show or hide.
##### Example
The illustration below shows you the column headings in the project's Commitments tool.
![Commitments Contracts Columns](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/f9af63c0c59e60851a9cc78985d3029d/e0a1caaf2f8680409ca7381cfaf55992/commitments-contracts-columns.png?fm=webp)
**Default Columns**
This table details the default columns in the **Contracts** tab of the project's Commitments tool.  
| Number  | Number  | Shows the commitment number.  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Contract Company  | Text  | Shows the name of the company responsible for completing the work for the commitment.  |  
| Title  | Text  | Shows the name entered in Procore for the commitment.  |  
| Status  | -  | Shows the current status of the commitment. To learn about the default statuses, see [What are the default commitment statuses in Procore?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-are-the-default-commitment-statuses-in-procore)  |  
| Executed  | Yes/No  | Shows whether or not the commitment has been executed.  |  
| Original Contract Amount  | Currency  | Shows the amount for the original commitment.  |  
| Approved Change Orders  | Currency  | Shows the amount of all change orders associated with the commitment currently in the 'Approved' status.  |  
| Revised Contract Amount  | Currency  | Shows the revised contract amount after any 'Approved' change orders.  |  
| Pending Change Orders  | Currency  | Shows the amount of all change orders in the 'Pending' status.  |  
| Draft Change Orders  | Currency  | Shows the amount of all change orders in the 'Draft' status.  |  
| Invoiced  | Currency  | Shows the amount that has been invoiced.  |  
| Payments Received  | Currency  | Shows the amount of any payments received.  |  
| % Paid  | Percentage  | Shows the percentage of the commitment amount that has been paid to date.  |  
| Remaining Balance Outstanding  | Currency  | Shows the remaining balance for the commitment. This value is calculated by subtracting the value of the amounts on the 'Payments Issued' tab of the invoice from the value in the 'Revised Contract Amount' cell. _Note:_ To learn about adding payments to the Payments Issued tab, see [Add a New Payment to the Payments Issued Tab of a Commitment](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/invoicing-project/tutorials/add-a-new-payment-to-the-payments-issued-tab-of-a-commitment)  |  
| Signatures Required  | User Name  | If you have enabled the Procore + [DocuSign®](https://support.procore.com/integrations/docusign) integration, this shows the name of the person from whom a signature is pending.  |  
| Private  | Yes/No  | Shows whether or not any privacy settings have been added to the commitment.  |  
1 If you have been assigned the required user permissions, you can edit the contents of a cell in this column inline.
**Additional Columns**
This table details the additional columns in the **Contracts** tab of the project's Commitments tool. These columns require specific features to be enabled.  
| SSOV Status  | Status  | When you [enable the Subcontractor SOV tab on a commitment](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/enable-or-disable-the-ssov-tab-on-a-commitment), shows the status of the Subcontractor Schedule of Values (SSOV).  |  
| --- | --- | --- |  
| Procore Pay Status  | Status  | When [Procore Pay is enabled](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/enable-procore-pay), shows the current status of the payee's bank account in the verification process. See What are the default statuses for Procore Pay onboarding?  |

title: What are the default columns in the Contracts tab of the Commitments tool?
description: Describes the columns that are available in the Contracts tab of the project's Commitments tool.
